title: Regional HSEQ Advisor
description:
Purpose of the Role

The role provides both technical and behavioral HSEQ support to the region while participating as an active member of the global HSEQ functional team. It involves offering HSEQ subject matter expertise to OSRL management and employees and acting as a mentor for those undertaking vocational HSEQ training. The position ensures regional alignment, consistency, and implementation of OSRL’s HSEQ policies, procedures, and processes. Additionally, it champions a generative and engaging HSEQ culture that encourages personal involvement and ownership of HSEQ, and develops and supports initiatives across Health, Safety, Environmental, and Quality areas to enhance OSRL’s capability and promote good practice.

Main Accountabilities

  1. Act as the region’s primary HSEQ representative and main point of contact with Group HSEQ, leading the implementation of new programs, processes, and standards within the region.
  2. Ensure consistent implementation of OSRL’s HSEQ IMS across the region and regularly review and update HSEQ procedures in the Blueprint to reflect industry best practices, regulatory changes, operational excellence, and learnings from incidents or near misses.
  3. Support the region in implementing effective risk management processes (such as hazard identification, risk assessment, and safe work procedures) for OSRL work, and mentor the Duty Safety Officers (DSOs) in the region.
  4. Ensure effective monitoring, reporting, and communication of HSEQ performance through established processes. Coordinate regional HSEQ Working Group meetings, including agenda support and broad participation across functions and locations. Support the maintenance and development of ASSURE, OSRL’s IMS records and document system. Encourage management and staff to take personal ownership of HSEQ.
  5. Support the development and implementation of HSEQ competency and training programs for both technical and non-technical staff, and assist Learning & Development with maintaining training records. Recommend appropriate numbers and skill levels for advanced or specialized training, and provide advice, training, and mentoring to regional personnel to help build a generative safety culture.
  6. Manage HSEQ interfaces with contractors and members/clients by ensuring contractors comply with OSRL’s HSEQ policies and procedures, acting as the primary contact for member HSEQ visits, audits, and information requests, and coordinating HSEQ support for OSRL staff during external operational or business visits.
  7. Ensure knowledge and compliance with industry good practices and regulatory requirements in countries where OSRL has bases / offices. This includes incident and ongoing regulatory reporting requirements. Remain up to date with new or changing legislation and draw attention to any implications for OSRL.
  8. Support regional assurance activities for external ISO certification, regulatory compliance, and internal adherence to the OSRL HSEQ IMS. Track and manage quality issues and audit findings through ASSURE, helping action owners close out actions promptly.
  9. Assure the timely and accurate reporting of all incidents, including near misses, support and /or undertake incident investigation, communicate investigation findings and implement recommendations.

Job Holder should hold safety related qualifications such as the Nebosh National or International Certificate (or similar equivalent). The Job Holder is expected to maintain CPD including by working towards higher qualifications (eg Nebosh Diploma or similar)

postedCompany.description:
Oil Spill Response Limited (OSRL) is the largest international industry-funded cooperative which exists to respond to oil spills wherever in the world they may occur, by providing preparedness, response and intervention services. We are wholly owned by our members and our membership represents the majority of global oil production. We currently employ 275 people across 12 locations around the world.


Our Mission

Our mission is to provide members with resources to prepare for and respond to oil spills efficiently and effectively globally to mitigate the impact on communities and the environment.


Our Vision

To lead the way, applying good practice and continuous innovation to spill response, adapting and evolving to meet our members' needs as they navigate the energy transition.


For more information, please visit our corporate website at: https://www.oilspillresponse.com/about-osrl/


We are a proud adopter of the Tripartite Standards. An initiative that distinguishes Singapore’s progressive employers.
postedCompany.name: OIL SPILL RESPONSE LIMITED
